_comment: How feedback nudges ranking. Borrowed from NewsBlur's additive 'intelligence' model: gestures add/subtract a small, bounded number of points; the LLM relevance score (0-100) still dominates ordering. 'skip' is implicit-negative: an item delivered to a user but not acted on within skip_days (no vote/save/click) — e.g. the builder agent reviewed it and opened no PR. Generalizes to any channel (email shown, never clicked). 'interest_weight' scales the two-tower interest match (z-scored cosine of a user's interest sentence to each item) into the same point space; 0 disables interest steering. 'explore_ratio' reserves that fraction of each user's top-N for EXPLORATION — a score-weighted stochastic pick among quality-gated candidates below the top — so the feed doesn't tunnel into one vein and the loop gathers feedback on under-seen items (0 = pure exploit; 0.2 on a top-5 = 1 wildcard slot).
